artifacts.claimText: Khalifa University Library maintains an Academic AI Tools guide that highlights tools supporting academic work such as discovering scholarly literature, organizing sources, drafting academic writing, and exploring emerging AI platforms, while aligning with library resources and best practices.

citation.publisher: Khalifa University of Science and Technology
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Khalifa University Library's AI tools guide lists several institutionally available or subscribed AI research tools, including LeapSpace, Primo AI Assistant, Scopus AI, ScienceDirect AI, EBSCO Research AI, and AL Manhal Arabic Platform AI.

artifacts.claimText: Khalifa University Library's Medical AI Research Guide says AI can help with medical research but advises users to document the exact AI questions, answers, platform, and timing when using AI to help searches.

artifacts.title: Khalifa University AI Policy Staged Source Review
artifacts.summary: Staged review found official Khalifa University Library guidance on academic AI tools and medical-research use of AI, plus general academic-integrity language in the 2024-25 Student Handbook. No official university-wide binding generative-AI policy was identified in this run.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The AI-specific claims are scoped to Khalifa University Library guides; the run did not identify a university-wide binding generative-AI policy.
The student-handbook claim is general academic-integrity context and does not mention generative AI.
